entry.title: how to Autostart NetWeaver on Sybase DB since sybctrl was removed from saproot.sh starting
entry.content:
In the past it was possible to start NetWeaver together with Sybase DB when executing startsap. Since startsap is not recommended by SAP and other security related changes, the startup of the sybase DB is not easy going.

According SAP Note 1902347 - Unable to start database with startsap - SAP ASE for Business Suite and following Notes for execution of startup (and shutdown) of the Sybase Database, Hostcontrol is recommended now ("sybctrl is removed from saproot.sh starting SAP Kernel 7.53").

I've figured out an example that you can use in crontab to enable autostart of Sybase DB (and NetWeaver) after system has booted and a shutdown procedure before system shutdown execution.

You have to create two text files in specific location that will be used by crontab for execution.

startup script file content:
date
sleep 5m
date
csh -c '/usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/saphostctrl -function StartDatabase -dbname <DBNAME> -dbtype syb'
date
sudo -H -u <sid>adm csh -c '/usr/sap/<SID>/SCS01/exe/sapcontrol -nr 01 -function StartSystem ALL'
date

shutdown script file content:
date
sudo -H -u <sid>adm csh -c '/usr/sap/<SID>/J00/exe/sapcontrol -nr 00 -function StopSystem ALL'
date
csh -c '/usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/saphostctrl -function StopDatabase -dbname <DBNAME> -dbtype syb'
date
sleep 1m
date
sudo su -c "shutdown now"

Remarks:

  • date is only for observing execution
  • enter your own SID instead of <SID> or <sid>
  • enter your own DB name instead of <DBNAME>

Crontab content:
# m h d M wd #
#45 10 * * * /scripts/startup > /scripts/startup.log
* 20 * * * /scripts/shutdown
@reboot /scripts/startup > /scripts/startup.log

Remark: crontab is created for root user.

Here you can see execution of autostart on our system:

Hopefully this is helpful for you, since it tooks me several triales and errors to solve that issue and became the system automatically started and stopped, since sybctrl was removed from saproot.sh.

Regards
Matthias

entry.title: How to Connect S4HANA Public Cloud With SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Author Biography: 

My name is Saikat Pal & I am a Manager in Data & Analytics practice of PwC India over 14+ years of experience in SAP Datasphere, SAP BTP HANA Cloud, SAP BTP CAPM, SAP BW4HANA, S4 HANA Embedded Analytics with CDS technology. I have exposure in techno-functional consulting in Material Management, Purchasing, Sales & Distribution, Customer Relationship  Management ( CRM ), Supply Chain Management ( SCM ) from Steel Making Industry, Pharma, Petrochemical, Cement, Solar etc.

Introduction:

After reading this article you will be able to establish a connection between SAP S4HANA Public Cloud with SAP Datasphere Public Cloud

Benefits:

      You will be able to replicate data from S4HANA Public Cloud to SAP Datasphere Public Cloud

Pre-requisites:

S4HANA Public Cloud user should have access to Maintain Communication Users, Communication Systems,Communication Arrangements app.

Steps for Configuration:
     

  • Log In to S4 HANA Public Cloud Tenant. Go to My Space

      

  • Search and select the “Maintain Communication Users” app​ from S4HANA Public Cloud

  • "Maintain Communication Users " app will open in S4HANA Public Cloud. Then click on New Button to create New User.

  • Put the User Name “YY1_USR_DS”

  • Put the User Name, Description, and Password as shown below and click “Create”​

  • Once Communication User is created, a CC* User ID will be generated​

  • Search and select the “Communication Systems” app and click on “New”​ to create new Communication System.

  • Give the System ID created earlier and the Host Name as "DWC"

  • Scroll down further and add User for Inbound Connection:​

  • Search the user that you have created earlier

  • Search and select the “Communication Arrangements” app and click “New”​

  • A pop-up box will appear with the following details

  • In the scenario, search for SAP_COMM_0532 and select it. Once selected give the Communication System which was created​

  • Once the Communication System is selected, scroll down for Authorization Group ID and select SAP_DPS_ALL​

  • Scroll down and identify the Inbound Communication and the Inbound Services. Review your inputs and click save.​

  • Now Log in to DataspherePlatform and click on Space Management -> Connections -> Go To Connections​

  • Select the Business Name and edit. Give the User Name and Password of Communication User and save​

  • Select the Business Name and validate. Please note that remote table was disabled as Data Provisioning Agent(DP Agent) was not installed at that time.

  • Now click on Data Builder -> New Data Flow​

 

 

  • Click on Sources from the Repository-Source option & you will be able to see S4HANA Cloud conneciton that you have created

 

Note: In this article Data Flow scenario is considered. You can also select Communication scenario SAP_COMM_0531 to replicate data via remote table.

entry.title: Difference between “multiattachment” and “multiattachmentselection” fields and their behavior
entry.content:
In SuccessFactors Recruiting Management (RCM), the “multiattachment” and “multiattachmentselection” fields are used for handling attachments in requisitions, candidate profiles, or job applications. Here's the key difference between the two:

File Types support: Commonly supported file types include DOC, DOCX, PDF, RTF, TXT, HTML, and various image formats (e.g., JPG, PNG).

Although both the field type serves the same purpose and that is to upload multiple document but the behaviour of these two are different.

  1. multiattachment:

It allows recruiting users to upload multiple attachments to a specific field and users can directly upload multiple files when you want to collect multiple documents from candidates such as resumes, cover letters or other documents, through a file upload interface.

The uploaded files are displayed as a list under the field where the attachments were made.

It is defined in XML templates with the field type as “multiattachment”, allowing multiple files to be uploaded directly to that field.

  1. multiattachmentselection:

It allows users/candidates to upload multiple attachments to a specific field and select multiple attachments from a predefined list or previously uploaded documents.

Instead of directly uploading new files, users/candidates can choose from a list of existing attachments, which might be uploaded in another part of the application or requisition process.

This field type is configured in XML templates as “multiattachmentselection” and involves specifying which documents are available for selection.

Summary:

“multiattachment” allows for new uploads, while “multiattachmentselection” is used for new uploads and also for selecting from existing files.

These fields allow for flexible handling of attachments in different parts of the recruiting process, tailored to specific needs like document upload or selection from available files.

 

In Candidate Profile:

Only “multiattachmentselection” field can be configured in the CPT and acts as a mini library where all documents uploaded are stored for later reuse. This means that there is an additional checkbox next to all uploaded files, and that checkbox must be selected to attach the document to the record.

If this field type is configured in the application with same “id” then the documents attached to the Candidate Profile will be copied into Application, when the candidate applies for a job.

And the Changes made on “multiattachmentselection” type of on application during the initial application are reflected to the field of type “multiattachmentselection” on Candidate Profile (But the field id should be same on both the template), but changes made to the application later during multi-stage applications will not sync back to the Candidate Profile.

 

In Job Application:

We can configure both “multiattachment” and “multiattachmentselection” fields in the application.

Only a recruiting user can upload documents for a field of “multiattachment” type, and they can also edit the field thereafter. However, candidates can’t view or upload documents for a field of this type. Multiattachmentselection needs to be used for candidates to view and upload documents.

For “multiattachmentselection”, both candidates and recruiting users can upload the documents if they have necessary write permissions. Most candidates aren't familiar with this field behaviour, so this field should not be used in a candidate-facing configuration. Because, documents uploaded to multiattachmentselection fields on the candidate profile are automatically available in a field with the same field ID on the application; also, the applicant can delete the attachment if desired.

Once a document has been added to the field, it can still be removed by the candidate through the external career site.

 

How to configure an attachment field which will be copied into application?

There are basically three ways for configuration of attachment fields:

  1. You can configure a field only in the Candidate Profile and not in the Application - the documents attached in the Candidate profile won't be copied to the application.
  2. You can configure an attachment field in the Candidate Profile template and a multiattachmentselection field in the Application template using the same field definition id - in this scenario, the documents attached to the Candidate Profile will be copied into Application, when the candidate applies for a job.
  3. You can define the attachment field only in the Application template - the candidate will be allowed to attach documents only during the application.

The multiattachmentselection field type should be given write permissions in both the Candidate Profile and Application Template for the sync to work as expected.

 

Notes:

Recruiting users can edit the field (add or remove), if they have “write” permissions on the Redesigned Workbench only.

Field definitions for the multiattachmentselection type cannot contain an underscore

You can configure an attachment field in the Candidate Profile template and a multiattachmentselection field in the Application template using the same field definition id - in this scenario, the documents attached to the Candidate Profile will be copied into Application, when the candidate applies for a job

entry.title: Streamlining Data Sharing: Emailing CSV Files from SAP Datasphere (DWC) using DIC
entry.content:
Introduction:

In today's data-driven world, extracting data from various sources and sharing it with stakeholders is a common requirement. Recently I had to work on a requirement that required me to extract a Datasphere table and send the extract as an attachment in an email through DIC. In this blog post, let’s explore how to extract a table from SAP Datasphere, convert it to a CSV file, and send it via email using the Send Email operator in SAP Data Intelligence Cloud. Let's dive in

Connection Setup:

Configure the SMTP connection in SAP Data Intelligence Cloud. This connection type required the following parameters: Host, Port, User Authentication, and Password of the SMTP connection.

Make sure you set up the connection properly to enable email sending functionality.

Creating the Graph:

To accomplish this task, Let us create graph consisting of the following components: Table Consumer, Python3 operator, Send Email operator, Wiretap (optional), and Graph Terminator.

  1. Table Consumer Operator: The table Consumer operator is configured to select the desired table from SAP Datasphere. This operator will fetch the required table for further processing.
  2. Python3 Operator: The output from the Table Consumer operator had to be converted into a message format. I used the 'Table to Message Converter' operator to achieve this conversion and connect its output to the input port of the Python3 operator.                                                                          Configure the input and output ports of the Python3 operator to handle messages.
  3. Python Code: Inside the Python3 operator, the following code snippet is used to convert the message into a CSV file and attach it to an email.
  4. Send Email Operator: Send Email operator is configured with the SMTP connection details. The output port of the Python3 operator was connected to the Send Email operator. This will pass the generated CSV file as an attachment to the email.
  5. Wiretap Operator (Optional): If you want to monitor or capture intermediate results, you can add a Wiretap operator. Connect the Send Email operator to the Wiretap operator and then to the Graph Terminator.

Executing the Graph: After configuring and connecting all the operators, save the graph and execute it. This will initiate the process of extracting the table, converting it to a CSV file, and sending it as an email attachment.

Conclusion: In this blog post, we learned how to leverage the power of SAP Data Intelligence Cloud to extract data from SAP Datasphere, convert it to a CSV file using Python, and send it via email using the Send Email operator. This functionality provides a seamless way to share data insights with stakeholders, enabling effective collaboration and decision-making. It's important to note that the provided Python code snippet is a basic example and may require modifications to suit specific use cases and environments.

entry.title: Side Effects in Fiori: Triggering Actions and Updating Data in Your Unmanaged RAP Application
entry.content:
Understanding Side Effects

Side Effects refer to system behaviors where a user’s action, such as modifying a field or performing a specific task, triggers changes in other parts of the user interface. For instance, updating a field might automatically adjust related fields or data sections. These changes are crucial for maintaining data consistency and ensuring that the user interface reflects the latest information.

User ActionSide Effect
Triggering an ActionAfter executing an action successfully, the collection for which the action is defined is refreshed automatically if the following conditions apply:
1. The action is a bound action.
2. The returned instance does not correspond to the bound instance. For example, copy actions.

In scenarios where side effects are not natively supported by the RAP layer, we can achieve similar functionality through Fiori annotations. Specifically, side effects can be implemented through action triggers and subsequent data refreshes in the Fiori layer.

Scenario Overview: Redetermine All Quotations

Consider a scenario where you have a button labeled "Redetermine All Quotations". Upon clicking this button, multiple columns in the item table are updated. For the purpose of this demonstration, we will focus on two key columns that are updated: Ref. Quotation and Quotation Items. The goal is to ensure that these updates are reflected immediately in the Fiori application without requiring manual refreshes.

 

Implementing Side Effects with Fiori Annotations

To address the requirement of real-time data updates, follow these steps:

  1. Open the Fiori Project in BAS: Navigate to the annotation.xml file within your Fiori project. This file initially displays the EDMX (Entity Data Model XML) structure.

  2. Add the Required Annotations: Insert the following code snippet into the annotation.xml file to configure side effects for your application. This annotation will ensure that the relevant data is refreshed automatically after executing an action.

     


    <edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
    <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
    <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Communication.xml">
    <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Communication.v1" Alias="Communication"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZUI_DRAFT_SALES_ORDERS/$metadata">
    <edmx:Include Namespace="cds_zui_draft_orders"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
    <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
    <Annotations Target="cds_zui_draft_orders.cds_zui_draft_orders_Entities/Refresh_items">
    <Annotation Term="Common.SideEffects">
    <Record Type="Common.SideEffectsType">
    <PropertyValue Property="TargetEntities">
    <Collection>
    <NavigationPropertyPath>_it/to_items</NavigationPropertyPath>
    </Collection>
    </PropertyValue>
    </Record>
    </Annotation>
    </Annotations>
    </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
    </edmx:Edmx>

  3. Test the Implementation: After updating the annotation file, return to the Fiori application. Open the developer tools and navigate to the network tab. Click the "Redetermine All Quotations" button, which triggers the action with the technical name refresh_items. You will observe a POST call for the action and a subsequent GET call to refresh the entire entity set of items as defined in the annotation.

     

  4. Verify Data Update: Check the data section of the items. You should see the updated values reflected immediately without the need for a manual refresh.

    Conclusion

    By leveraging Fiori annotations to implement side effects and action triggers, you can ensure that your unmanaged RAP application remains responsive and up-to-date. This approach effectively addresses the limitations of the RAP layer and enhances the overall user experience by maintaining data consistency in real-time.
    Feel free to share your comments and feedback. This approach is designed to keep your data up-to-date in real-time, ensuring a smooth and dynamic experience for your users.

entry.title: 3 Spook-Proof Strategies For A Resilient Halloween Supply Chain
entry.content:
According to the National Retail Federation, Halloween spending reached a record $12.2 billion last year, surpassing the previous high of $10.6 billion. This figure encompasses a wide range of expenditures, including costumes, decorations, greeting cards, and candy.

“More Americans than ever will be reaching into their wallets and spending a record amount of money to celebrate Halloween this year,” said NRF President and CEO Matthew Shay from a recent news release. “Consumers will be shopping early for festive décor and other related items and retailers are prepared with the inventory to help customers and their families take part in this popular and fun tradition.”

Here’s how forward-thinking brands will meet overwhelming consumer demand this Halloween season.

 

#1 Early planning is the secret ingredient for supply chain resilience

Successful Halloween campaigns hinge on meticulous forecasting of consumer demand, often requiring brands to make decisions 18 months to two years in advance. This involves determining which costumes, decorations, and candies to produce and the quantities needed. 

Retailers are beginning their sourcing efforts earlier than ever, starting preparations about a month ahead of last year. This proactive approach allows them to align production with anticipated trends. For instance, the popularity of the Barbie movie last year made Barbie and Ken costumes top choices.

However, rapid shifts in consumer preferences can create mismatches between expectations and available inventory. The upcoming horror films like the new Beetlejuice, Crow or Joker movies may influence consumer choices just weeks before Halloween. Or it’s not that hard to anticipate a surge in demand for Trump or Kamala costumes with the upcoming U.S. elections.

Overproduction remains a significant risk in supply chain management. Producing goods over actual demand can lead to significant waste and lost revenue. If companies produce too many and the holiday passes, they will be left with unsold inventory that has little to no value after the season ends.

To capitalize on seasonal demand without the burden of long-term leases, some retailers are establishing temporary stores across the U.S., including in unconventional locations like abandoned strip malls. This strategy optimizes logistics for setting up and dismantling pop-up stores.

 

#2 Efficient logistics and shipping operations are essential

Companies have started shipping Halloween products about a month ahead of last year to avoid delays caused by ongoing logistical challenges. Increasing shipping costs, like port congestion and labor strikes still affect timelines.

Container freight rates slumped to their lowest level last October. Since then, rates have increased by more than 330% per container, reaching record highs. In response, companies are proactively looking for optimal ways of managing their supply chains to ensure timely delivery.

A growing trend is the shift of production closer to home, with many companies moving manufacturing from China to Mexico. With nearshoring, they aim to reduce dependency on distant supply chains and mitigate risks associated with international shipping such as lead times and transportation costs. This not only enhances responsiveness but also improves communication and oversight of production processes, enabling supply chains to be more agile and resilient.

 

#3 Prioritising proactive and innovative approaches to navigating challenges is crucial for managing disruptions

Building resilience is essential for companies to effectively navigate supply chain disruptions, such as climate change, natural disasters, and geopolitical tensions.

Multisourcing is one of the resilience strategies that companies are increasingly adopting. By diversifying suppliers, businesses can procure raw materials from multiple sources, ensuring a consistent flow of materials even if one supplier encounters challenges.

Leveraging artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML), companies can enhance supply chain visibility and decision-making processes. Real-time data analysis allows companies to anticipate disruptions and respond quickly, fostering better collaboration among suppliers. In an era marked by uncertainty, collaboration and trust with key suppliers are vital. Engaging suppliers in joint planning and risk management also contributes to enhancing the overall supply chain robustness.

As Halloween approaches, brands are navigating a complex landscape of supply chain challenges, requiring them to be proactive, flexible, and innovative in their strategies. Brands that prioritize early planning, efficient logistics, and resilient supply chain strategies will be well-positioned to thrive in this competitive landscape, ensuring they meet consumer demand while minimizing waste and maximizing profitability.

To discover key strategies for building risk-resilient supply chains, download the Oxford Economics Report: “5 Supply Chain Strategies to Achieve Resiliency and Avoid Risk”

entry.title: Re: Adding / changing printer for Manage Physical Invento...
entry.content:
Hello @Ashali,

App Manage Physical Inventory Documents also provides the Print options as below.

  • Background Print: Printing of several physical inventory documents via background job: Printing of all physical inventory documents that are included in the list on the list level. This selection will then be considered for the print job based on selectable settings. The print results can be checked in the app  .

  • Print Document: Printing of one complete physical inventory document: all items are considered for the print. The result can be checked in the Output Management facet.

  • Print Items: Printing of selected items of a physical inventory document: selected items are considered for the print. The result can be checked in the Output Management facet.

Refer to the app documentation as well. https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0379A')/S33 

Best Regards,

Vijayendra

entry.title: Re: send xml file as attachment
entry.content:
TRY.
cl_bcs_convert=>string_to_solix(
EXPORTING
iv_string = lv_string_f "lw_string
iv_codepage = '4103'
iv_add_bom = abap_true
IMPORTING
et_solix = ltb_solix
ev_size = lw_size ).
lw_subject = 'Taiwan PTCS Plan list(274i)'.
lw_atch_sub = '274I report result'.
lob_send_request = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).

lob_document = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
i_type = 'RAW'
i_text = ftb_body
i_subject = lw_subject ).

"Add TXT DOC to email's attachment
CALL METHOD lob_document->add_attachment(
EXPORTING
i_attachment_type = 'TXT'
i_attachment_subject = lw_atch_sub
i_attachment_size = lw_size
i_att_content_hex = ltb_solix
).
"Add mail to send
lob_send_request->set_document( lob_document ).
LOOP AT p_ltb_recip INTO lwa_recipient.
lw_address = lwa_recipient.
lob_recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address(
i_address_string = lw_address ).
"" Sender as sap user
lob_send_request->add_recipient( i_recipient = lob_recipient ).
ENDLOOP.


lob_send_user = cl_sapuser_bcs=>create( sy-uname ).
lob_send_request->set_sender( lob_send_user ).

"Send email.
lob_send_request->set_send_immediately( abap_true ).
lob_send_request->send( i_with_error_screen = abap_true ).


CATCH cx_bcs INTO lob_bcs_exception.
CALL METHOD lob_bcs_exception->if_message~get_text
RECEIVING
result = lw_message.

MESSAGE lw_message TYPE 'W'.
ENDTRY.

COMMIT WORK.

entry.title: Form - Regular Expression Input Validation Message
entry.content:
Hi,

We built a Form in SAP Marketing Cloud, and wanted to apply extra validation to First Name, that it should have at least 2 letters.

We used the box "Regular Expression" for it with ^[a-zA-A]{2,}$

Technically this works fine, however when we try to submit the form with a first name with one single character, a validation message shows "The input must match the specified regular expression ^[a-zA-A]{2,}$". Not a very nice User Experience of course, and meaningless to most people 🙂

On other fields with a standard validation such as email, there is a box for Input Validation Message, where the custom error message can be written.

However, on First Name, where I applied the RegEx, I cannot seem to find an Input Validation Message box ...

Is it not possible to overwrite that technical validation message when using RegEx?!

 

Kind regards,

Joyca Vervinckt

entry.title: BBQ, Beer and Worker Rights: Labor Day Supply Chain
entry.content:
Today is Labor Day, a moment when we fire up the grill, crack open a cold one, and pay tribute to the hardworking individuals who keep our economy thriving. As the unofficial end of summer approaches, families and friends across the nation are gearing up for the ultimate backyard grilling, stocking up on burgers, brats, and beverages for this special occasion.

But behind the scenes, major retailers and businesses nationwide work tirelessly to ensure our shelves are stocked.

 

Sizzling strategies for the Labor Day rush

A recent survey reveals that 80% of Americans plan to host or attend a barbecue during Labor Day weekend. Grilling or not, 90% plan to purchase food, and over half plan to purchase alcoholic beverages. To cater to this demand, supermarkets are making strategic preparations and implementing several strategies. They are:

- leveraging advanced analytics to analyze sales data from previous years to determine the ideal quantities of grilling equipment helps businesses forecast the ideal quantities of grilling essentials. Predicting demand at the day-SKU-store level, enables them to position inventory more effectively, minimizing waste and maximizing sales opportunities.

- expanding inventory for grilling essentials including different cuts of meat, plant-based options, and long-shelf-life items like hotdogs and frozen buns. This strategy not only caters to diverse consumer preferences but also ensures that businesses can meet the rising demand without running into stock shortages.

- building strong relationships with suppliers to ensure a reliable and efficient flow of goods, as it is essential for alternating sourcing options and expediting delivery services in case of potential disruptions. So retailers can act proactively to find alternative sourcing options, expedite deliveries, and provide real-time information to help businesses adapt in an agile manner.

- leveraging just-In-Time (JIT) inventory management to receive products based on actual sales rather than forecasts. This helps retailers reduce the need for large storage spaces and reduces the risk of spoilage for perishable goods. By using real-time insights into stock levels, retailers can dynamically order based on actual sales trends. If a particular item is selling faster than anticipated, supermarkets can quickly place additional orders with suppliers to restock, ensuring that they do not run out of popular items during the holiday weekend.

- using omnichannel integration by integrating their online and offline channels. They can provide an easy shopping experience for customers such as offering curbside pickup and same-day delivery.

- building effective communication and collaboration among all stakeholders in the supply chain to enhance operational efficiency and responsiveness, especially during the peak seasons. They can facilitate real-time data sharing across departments and various stakeholders, so everyone to reach the same information enabling quicker decision-making and more agile response to changes in demand.

 

The importance of prioritizing employees in the supply chain

Upholding employee rights and ethical standards is continuously increasing as consumers, investors and governments demand transparency and accountability in supply chain practices. Laws like Germany's Supply Chain Act enforce due diligence in respecting human rights throughout the supply chain, protecting against issues like forced labor and discrimination. Companies that proactively manage these risks can avoid reputational damage and legal implications that arise from labor violations.

And of course, it fosters trust. Prioritizing employee rights fosters a culture of trust and accountability. When employees are treated fairly and their rights are respected, it leads to higher job satisfaction, reduced turnover, and better overall performance, which benefits the entire supply chain. Leaders who model ethical behavior and hold themselves accountable create an environment where employees feel valued and motivated.

By focusing on the rights of workers and communities, we can achieve sustainable development, create positive social impacts, and build a more equitable and inclusive future.

On this special day, let’s take a well-deserved break and reflect on the supply chain heroes who keep our shelves full, and supply chains running. From the truck drivers logging countless miles to deliver our goods, to the warehouse workers tirelessly picking and packing orders, to the dock workers loading and unloading cargo - you are the backbone of our economy.

Are you ready to transform your supply chain into a resilient and sustainable powerhouse? Download the Oxford Economics Report: “5 Supply Chain Strategies to Achieve Resiliency and Avoid Risk”

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP S/4HANA Invoicing with KSeF Using SAP Event Mesh and CAP
entry.content:
Hi Gregor,

I think the idea of using an integration flow is nice, but honestly, I'm not sure if it’s feasible (perhaps you can share your experience on this). My concern is based on the fact that integration with KSeF is far more complicated than described in the post. To send an invoice, you need to follow these steps:

1)Request a timestamp and authorization challenge.
2)Calculate another timestamp as the difference between 1/1/1970 and the timestamp you received.
3)Encrypt (using RSA) a combination of your authorization token (each organization registered in KSeF has its own token. The token could be either a general token for the organization or a personal token for each employee with different permissions) and the timestamp you calculated in step 2.
4)Send the encrypted token along with the authorization challenge to initiate the session and obtain the session token.
5)Send your invoice using the session token.
6)Send one more request to confirm that the invoice was registered.
Honestly, I'm not an expert in integration flows. Do you think it would be possible to perform all these steps using this tool?

KR,
Yahor

entry.title: Re: SAP FICA - Clarification processing through FPMA
entry.content:
Hi Ram,

FPMA is meant for open item clearing, debit and credit open items which are posted at contract account level can be cleared using FPMA.

Now, clarification cases are items which are not posted at contract account level. Moreover, each and every clarification case will have different clarification to be done like incorrect contract account, clearing not permitted, contract account blocked etc.

There is not such standard option for automatic clarification.

Regards,

Mitesh

entry.title: Re: Single dunning letter for multiple company codes at same dunning level
entry.content:
Hi Freeman,

Have you tried using a single dunning company code. Dunning Company Codes are exactly meant for that.

Copying the field help:

If you wish to use cross-company code dunning, you must assign the company codes that you wish to include to a dunning company code. The dunning company code is assigned to itself.

Use

You can use cross-company code dunning to dun overdue items from several company codes at the same time. The due items from accounts that exist in several company codes are dunned with one dunning notice.

The items are grouped according to pre-defined rules (by dunning level, dunning area, dunning grouping) and assigned to one or more dunning notices. This eliminates the need to send a customer a separate dunning notice for each company code.

Procedure

Enter the key of the dunning company code that you wish to assign to the company code in question. You must also make this entry for the dunning company code.

 

Dependencies

If a date has been specified for an account in the dunning company code which marks it for inclusion in the legal dunning procedure, this also affects the dependent company codes.

However, dunning frequency and dunning blocks are checked separately for each company code.

You customize dunning company codes and assignments in T047 Tcode (same as table :)).

I hope this helps.

BR,

Filipe

entry.title: How integrated Project Cost and Revenue Planning helps to keep your promises to clients
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's manufacturing and engineering business, overseeing customer projects from the bidding stage through execution and delivery can be a challenging task.  From the initial stages of project conception to the final delivery, companies must navigate a labyrinth of variables to ensure projects are completed on time, within budget, and to the satisfaction of their clients.

SAP's Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management (EPPM) solution addresses these challenges. By providing an integrated approach to cost and revenue planning, SAP EPPM helps manufacturing companies gain real-time visibility into project performance, enabling them to make informed decisions and maintain control over project execution.

This blog will illustrate how SAP EPPM can transform your project management processes, ensuring seamless integration of project schedules with cost and revenue plans.

How Sarah keeps her promise to clients  !

Sarah is a project manager at a leading manufacturing company for solar technologies. She is responsible for overseeing a new large project to install solar panels for a major client. The project is complex, involving multiple teams, long lead-time materials, and tight deadlines. Sarah knows that meticulous planning is crucial for the project's success.

Sarah receives a draft project start and end date based on the project schedule agreed with the customer in the sales contract. She starts detailing out the project timeline entering expected duration of network activities, creating dependencies and scheduling project milestones. To plan and structure the project she uses the embedded project management module SAP Project System.

Image: Review project network activities in SAP Project System

As a next steps Sarah creates a project cost and revenue plan based on the project structure and timeline she has defined in the step before. At this point she can specify the demand for external and internal workers, material components, and external services needed for each work package in the project. For instance, she identifies the need for 85 units of Solar Panel (320KW) to be procured between July and October each costing 5000 EUR.

Image: Financial Cost and Revenue planning in EPPM - Commercial Project Management 

This detailed planning of material demands includes expected costs rates, required quantities and the revenue rates for each material and planning period. With a click on the “Valuate” button she can trigger to automatically derive material cost and revenue rates from various sources in SAP S/4HANA such as product master data and sales order information.

Image: Initiate handover of project financial planning into controlling 

Sarah develops multiple versions of the project plan accommodating budget and resource availability while optimizing overall project profitability.

Once Sarah has finalized the planning, she releases the current version to be transferred into finance and controlling module in SAP S/4HANA. This creates an active cost and revenue plan used by project controllers to monitor progress and cost performance, and to trigger invoicing and billing processes.

Sarah uses the Project Cost Status report in EPPM to track actual costs, quantities and costs rates against the current plan and baseline plan. In addition she can compare these values against the estimation from the latest forecast (ETC - Estimation to Completion / EAC -  Estimation At Completion)    

Image: Detailed Costs and Revenue Reporting based on Project Structure  

Transferring the cost and revenue plan into project controlling also updates the material demand information in the project. For example, the required 85 units  for Solar Panel ML-SPANEL, including availability dates and cost information, are transferred into the component demand details managed in the Project System.

 

Image: Project Demand Details in SAP Project System derived from the integrated cost and revenue plan

The logistic information stored in SAP Project System now serves as the trigger for procurement and production planning, automatically creating material purchase requisitions with reservations. This ensures that once the material is delivered and added to stock, it is reserved for the specific project and not consumed by other projects.

Image : Details of the material purchase orders generated out of SAP Project System

One of the key benefits Sarah experiences with this integrated approach is the ability to forecast material and resource demands accurately based on the underlying project structure and timeline, keeping both processes in synch with each other,

E.g. for materials with long lead times, such as in this case solar panels, the demand derived from the project plan is included in the long-term parts and resources planning provided by Integrated Business Planning for the Maintenance, repair, and operations solution. This ensures that all demands, whether from maintenance orders or individual customer projects, are visible in a single supply plan. This structured approach reduces errors and ensures all financial and procurement data is received from the demanding sources and up-to-date.

Sarah's team benefits from early insights into material demands, avoiding delays and cost overruns. The procurement team can create purchase requisitions and orders based on the information derived from the cost and revenue plan. This well-coordinated effort between procurement, project management, and supply chain teams ensures that materials are procured at expected prices and are available on-site when needed.

Outro

In conclusion, SAP's Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management solution provides a comprehensive, integrated approach to managing project costs and revenues.
By leveraging this powerful tool, Sarah and her team can ensure that all aspects of their project are aligned, from financial planning to material procurement and labor management.
This improves the project’s performance and drives business value by reducing errors, avoiding delays, and ensuring projects are completed on time and within budget.

With SAP EPPM, manufacturing companies can navigate the complexities of project management with confidence and precision and such keep their promises that they gave to their customers.

entry.title: FINS_REC* jobs
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support,

After implemented note 3036084, would the FINS_REC* jobs be automatically cancelled?

My second question.... After implementing note 3036084, how do I know if I need to run FINS_REC analysis or not? Can you please clarify? 

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: FINS_REC* jobs
entry.content:
After the note: 3036084 is implemented:

 

  1.  If your ACDOCA table has more than 1 billion(1.000.000.000) entries, FINS_REC* jobs will not be triggered; 
  2.  If your ACDOCA table has less than 1 billion entries, FINS_REC* jobs will still be triggered. 
  3.  In the situation of ACDOCA table with less than 1 billion entries, and you would like to stop triggering FINS_REC* jobs, it is possible after implementing the following SAP Note:
    Note 3093945 - Financial Data Quality analysis FINS_REC in Early Watch Alert

 

FINS_REC analyses Finance data inconsistencies. If there are FI inconsistencies related to balance mismatch in accounts, differences in database table level, etc and business wants to perform analysis on the cause of the FI inconsistencies in the SAP S/4 HANA Environment, they can consider running FINS_REC.

See following KBA for more information:

3478443 - How to perform basic checks for FI inconsistencies on SAP S/4 HANA System

entry.title: Batch Award Nominations
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

Our client requires us to create a batch nomination id for Team Awards. This award is given to multiple people who worked on a project, for example, and hence there will be instances wherein this award will be given to 20 or even up to 50 recipients at a time. This is essential in tracking these awards in reports.

We are able to create a business rule with this format and store it in a custom field within the Spot Award object itself:

TeamAward_<NominatorID>_<Date>

However what the client requires should look something like this:

TeamAward_<NominatorID>_<Date>_<BatchID> , wherein this BatchID should be the same for the batch nomination of those 20 to 50 recipients. The first solution did not work since there will be times wherein the Nominator has to create multiple batch of nominations per day.

However, using the "Sequence" object, it generates unique ids for each of the award which is not correct. Using autonumber would also have the same problem.

Has anyone encounter a similar requirement for other clients?

 

 

Many thanks!

Karelyn

entry.title: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
Sometimes, starting a business transformation on your own can be daunting.

We understand the challenge, which is why we’d like to give everyone joining this group an opportunity to get to know each other better and to make the most out of our collective knowledge and support in this group by fostering authentic human connections. 

To kick things off, we'd love for YOU to introduce yourself so members of this group can get to know you better and start connecting: 

  1. What is your current role and responsibilities in your company? 
  2. What are your reasons for joining this community group? What do you hope to find or achieve here? 
  3. What are your strengths that you can share and support others? 
  4. Which solutions do you use for transforming your organization (e.g. SAP Signavio Process Manager, SAP LeanIX Enterprise Architecture, etc.)? 
  5. What’s your level of expertise using SAP Signavio (or other SAP solutions)? 
  6. What has been your favorite/proudest accomplishment with SAP Signavio (or other SAP solutions) so far? 


Feel free to leave a comment below with your introduction. We can’t wait to hear from you and start this journey of transformation together!

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
I’ll go first! 

Hi everyone, I'm Shreenith Naik. I serve as the Senior Community Manager for the SAP Signavio Community within the Customer Success team at SAP Signavio. My role is all about ensuring you have the best possible community experience and providing steady support in your business transformation journey with SAP Signavio. 

I first starting using SAP Signavio about four years ago, during my onboarding phase in the height of the pandemic in 2020. Back then, I completed my onboarding tasks using the old Signavio Workflow Accelerator, now known as SAP Signavio Process Governance. I remember thinking that it was the most interactive way to learn the product, adapt to the company culture, and get to know the teams all at once. One of my fondest memories is completing a creative task with a picture of me and my 'Siggy' (a Signavio stuffed toy given to newbies) and sharing it with our colleagues. That right there was my first and most memorable memories with Signavio, both with the product and the team and I've been using SAP Signavio ever since.

Now it’s your turn! 
I look forward to hearing your stories.😊

entry.title: Hear from SAP Champion Kunal Bansal 🏅: How SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 Can Help Boost Your Career
entry.content:
When Kunal booked his hotel in Bangalore near the 2023 SAP TechEd venue, he knew he wouldn’t be spending much time there. Instead, his real focus was on the learning, networking, and upskilling at SAP’s premier technology event - TechEd. “Learn, share, grow - that’s the motto I believe in,” Kunal says, reflecting on his experience. Attending SAP TechEd in-person for the 2nd time, he discovered just how powerful in-person networking and knowledge-sharing could be. “It’s the best place to connect, ask questions, and exchange ideas,” Kunal emphasizes. 

His enthusiasm shines through as he recalls the inspiring keynote delivered by SAP Board Members Jürgen Müller and Julia White, which focused on the transformative potential of GenAI. He also cherishes the time spent at the SAP Community booth, engaging with fellow SAP Mentors and Champions. “I’m definitely attending again this year,” he says with a smile. 

With SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 on October 8-9 just weeks away, Kunal is busy curating his agenda. He’s carefully selecting sessions that will provide the most value, both professionally and personally. 

Kunal's Top Picks for SAP TechEd Virtual in 2024:

  • XP101 | SAP BTP strategy and highlights  
    Get firsthand insights into SAP’s BTP strategy and gain a deeper understanding of how SAP BTP unifies development, automation, data, and AI into one powerful platform, driving digital transformation. 
  • IN100 | Innovate now with a modern, AI-assisted integration suite 
    Discover how to accelerate innovation by integrating business systems using SAP Integration Suite, enabling real-time, scalable enterprise-wide integration.  
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite 
    Learn how to transition smoothly from legacy systems to the SAP cloud, leveraging SAP Integration Suite’s robust tools, best practices and services for a seamless migration. 

Make sure you also check out Kunal's entire expert session playlist for SAP TechEd Virtual!

SAP Learning site is his go-to resource

“Learn, share, grow - that’s the motto I believe in,” Kunal says.During the week, Kunal is fully immersed in his role as an Advisory Architect at IBM, leveraging over 13 years of experience in SAP CX Suites. His deep expertise in SAP Intelligent Enterprise and SAP Solutions underscores his commitment to staying current in the field. “I work closely with stakeholders to deliver scalable, compliant, and high-performing solutions using SAP technologies,” Kunal explains. His role demands a unique blend of technical expertise, strategic thinking, and strong communication skills to drive successful project outcomes. “And to stay current, I dedicate at least an hour daily to learning.” 

Kunal often turns to the SAP Learning site to stay updated with the rapidly evolving technologies. “The free SAP Learning portal has been my go-to resource for learning journeys, leading to SAP Certification, while Learning Hub and Partner Edge portals have deepened my concepts to deliver greater business value for clients”, Kunal says. 

Strengthened credibility, solidified reputation

While dedicating an hour a day to learning can seem daunting at first, Kunal says it’s worth it, as it has become a crucial part of his journey, pushing him to achieve greater heights and inspiring others. “Earning SAP Certifications through continuous learning has been a game-changer for my career growth, unlocking opportunities for more complex, high-impact projects”. On a more personal level, Kunal notes, “SAP Certifications have strengthened my credibility and solidified my reputation as a thought leader in the SAP Community.” 

As Kunal finalizes his SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 agenda, he’s also exploring the recently launched SAP TechEd Virtual Learning Zone to discover further learning tools and opportunities mapped to the six SAP TechEd Virtual content tracks. “I’m excited to dive into Developing with SAP Integration Suite to keep myself engaged until SAP TechEd Virtual kicks off,” Kunal says, eagerly anticipating the innovations in SAP Business AI and SAP BTP. 

Now it's your time to shine!

What sessions are you most excited about, and what’s your favorite resource from the SAP TechEd Virtual - Learning Zone? Share your thoughts in the comments and make sure to visit the SAP TechEd group on SAP Community to connect with peers!

entry.title: Call REST service in CAP project using destination with OAuth2JWTBearer
entry.content:
Hi community,

lately I tried to connect a REST Service to a CAP project in order to put a fiori elements app on top of the REST service.
My solution was to proxy the data according to this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/convert-rest-to-odata-through-a-proxy-service-using-cap/ba-p/13553033

It seems to work for destinations with NoAuthentication. The destination I want to use has the authentication OAuth2JWTBearer. 
Trying to call the service results in the error message: Error during request to remote service: Failed to load destination. Caused by: No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2JWTBearer'.

Is there a solution to firstly get a token and then pass it in every request using this service?
At the moment I did not master it to retrieve any data from the destination.

If there is missing any information, feel free to ask.
Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Will firewall affects while connecting to database table via SAP CPI ?
entry.content: You need to provide more details about your setup: Which Databases, where are they located exactly?

entry.title: Re: Call REST service in CAP project using destination with OAuth2JWTBearer
entry.content: Does the REST service you're using have a public documentation? Who is triggering the Service call? If it isn't initiated by a user no User Token will be available. Then you need to check if you can use OAuth Client Credentials instead.
